perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall implement and maintain appropriate security measures for Personal Data which shall be at least as protective of the confidentiality of such information as the safeguards for personal information set forth under applicable federal, state and local law .
perturbation.changed_text:
Recipient shall implement and maintain appropriate security measures for Personal Data which shall be at least as protective of the confidentiality of such information as the safeguards for personal information set forth under applicable federal, state and local law .
18. Interpretation . In the Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires, words describing the singular nu mber shall include the plural and vice versa, and words denoting any gender shall include all genders, and words denoting natural persons shall include corporations and partnerships and vice versa. Whenever the words “include,” “includes,” or “including” are used in the Agreement, they shall be understood to be followed by the words “without limitation.” The words “hereof,” “herein,” and “hereunder,” and words of similar import, when used in the Agreement refer to the Agreement as a whole and not to any p articular provision of the Agreement unless otherwise specified. All terms used herein with initial capital letters have the meanings ascribed to them herein, and all terms defined in the Agreement shall have such defined meanings when used in any certifi cate or other document made or delivered pursuant hereto, unless otherwise defined therein. The definitions contained in the Agreement are applicable to the singular as well as the plural forms of such terms and to the masculine as well as to the feminine and neuter genders of such term. Any agreement, instrument, or statute defined or referred to herein, or in any agreement or instrument that is referred to herein, means such agreement, instrument, or statute as from time to time amended, modified, or su pplemented, including (in the case of agreements or instruments) by waiver or consent and (in the case of statutes) by succession of comparable successor statutes and references to all attachments thereto and instruments incorporated therein. The headings used in this Agreement are merely for reference and impose no obligation nor have any substantive significance. Each of the Parties has participated in the drafting and negotiation of the Agreement. If any ambiguity or question of intent or interpretatio n arises, the Agreement must be construed as if it is drafted by all the Parties and no presumption or burden of proof shall arise favoring or disfavoring any Party by virtue of authorship of any of the provisions of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the PII security requirements into the 'Interpretation' section makes it less prominent and harder to find, potentially weakening its enforceability. This is because data security obligations should be clearly outlined and easily accessible, not buried within a general interpretive clause.
perturbation.law_explanation: M.G.L. c. 93H, § 3 requires businesses that own or license personal information of Massachusetts residents to develop, implement, and maintain a comprehensive information security program. By moving the clause regarding security measures for Personal Data to the 'Interpretation' section, the agreement obscures the explicit security requirements, potentially violating the spirit and intent of Massachusetts law, which mandates that such measures be clearly and conspicuously defined.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: General Law - Part I, Title XV, Chapter 93H, Section 3 Skip to Content General Laws Part I ADMINISTRATION OF THE GOVERNMENT Title XV REGULATION OF TRADE Chapter 93H SECURITY BREACHES Section 3 Duty to report known security breach or unauthorized use of personal information Section 3 Search Search the Legislature Search General Laws Go Directly to a General Law Chapter Section Go to General Law Skip to Content Print Page Prev ious Next Section 3: Duty to report known security breach or unauthorized use of personal information Section 3. (a) A person or agency that maintains or stores, but does not own or license data that includes personal information about a resident of the commonwealth, shall provide notice, as soon as practicable and without unreasonable delay, when such person or agency (1) knows or has reason to know of a breach of security or (2) when the person or agency knows or has reason to know that the personal information of such resident was acquired or used by an unauthorized person or used for an unauthorized purpose, to the owner or licensor in accordance with this chapter. In addition to providing notice as provided herein, such person or agency shall cooperate with the owner or licensor of such information. Such cooperation shall include, but not be limited to, informing the owner or licensor of the breach of security or unauthorized acquisition or use, the date or approximate date of such incident and the nature thereof, and any steps the person or agency has taken or plans to take relating to the incident, except that such cooperation shall not be deemed to require the disclosure of confidential business information or trade secrets, or to provide notice to a resident that may have been affected by the breach of security or unauthorized acquisition or use. (b) A person or agency that owns or licenses data that includes personal information about a resident of the commonwealth, shall provide notice, as soon as practicable and without unreasonable delay, when such person or agency (1) knows or has rea

perturbation.justification: The modified contract text introduces a structural flaw by moving the PII security requirements into the 'Interpretation' section, making it less prominent and harder to find. This undermines the enforceability of the data security obligations because data security obligations should be clearly outlined and easily accessible. The law explanation argues this change potentially violates M.G.L. c. 93H, § 3, which requires businesses to maintain a comprehensive information security program. While the snippets do not explicitly state where the security measures must be placed within a contract, snippet 1 does reference chapter 93H, section 3, giving the law explanation credibility that this law is about data security and breaches. Moving the clause makes it less clear and conspicuous, thus weakening its legal force.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one ( 1) year from the Effective Date. This Agreement may be terminated by either pa rty upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. Termination or expiration of this Agreement shall not relieve either party of its obligations hereunder to maintain in confidence and not to use Confidential Information received hereunder for a period of three (3) years from the date of receipt thereof.
perturbation.changed_text:
The term of this Agreement shall be one ( 1) year from the Effective Date.
1. Confidential Information . “Confidential Information ” means all the information of the Disclosing Party furnish ed or made available , orally, in writing, by electronic means, or by inspection, at any time on and after the Effective Date regarding the Disclosing Party or its business plans , processes, technology, data, material, documents regarding the infrastructure, businesses, assets, liabilities, financial conditions, operations, identity of personnel, inte llectual property (including, without limitation, trade secrets), Personal Data (defined below ) and other related or its similar information provided in all cases such information is marked conspicuously with a confidentiality or a substantially similar le gend in accordance with Section 4 of this Agreement . Termination or expiration of this Agreement shall not relieve either party of its obligations hereunder to maintain in confidence and not to use Confidential Information received hereunder for a period of three (3) years from the date of receipt thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By placing the confidentiality obligation in the definition of confidential information instead of within the clause about the term of the agreement, the agreement makes the clause less obvious and therefore undermines its purpose. This relocation creates uncertainty as to when the confidentiality obligation begins.
perturbation.law_explanation: M.G.L. c. 93A prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices in trade or commerce. Obscuring the duration and termination clause, including the continued confidentiality obligations after termination, could be viewed as a deceptive practice as it makes it difficult for a party to fully understand their responsibilities and the timeline of those responsibilities. This could potentially lead to legal challenges under Chapter 93A.

perturbation.justification: The modified text alters the original contract by placing the confidentiality obligation within the definition of 'Confidential Information' instead of alongside the term and termination clauses. This shift could obscure the duration and scope of the confidentiality obligations, making it less clear when these obligations begin and end. This obscurity potentially creates a deceptive practice under M.G.L. c. 93A, as the Law Explanation states, because it may be difficult for a party to fully understand their responsibilities. Scraped Law Snippet 1 and 2 both reference Chapter 93A, which regulates business practices for consumer protection and addresses unfair practices. The structural flaw of moving the confidentiality obligation undermines its clarity and enforceability, supporting the determination of a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Should the Recipient become aware of any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, the Recipient agrees to promptly notify the Disclosing Party in writing and , in addition to the other rights set forth in this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall have the right to immediately terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The validity, interpretation, and effect of this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. The Parties hereby e xpressly consent to the exclusive personal jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in the Commonwealth of Massachusetts for any dispute arising from or relating to this Agreement. If any term or condition of this Agreement shall be determined by any court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid or unenforceable to any extent, the remainder of this Agreement shall not be affected thereby, and each remaining term or condition hereof shall be valid and enforced to the fullest extent permitted by law. In the event such determination prevents the accomplishment of the purpose of this Agreement, the invalid provision shall be restated to conform with applicable law and to reflect as nearly as possible the original intention of the Parties. Should the Recipient become aware of any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, the Recipient agrees to promptly notify the Disclosing Party in writing and , in addition to the other rights set forth in this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall have the right to immediately terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the notification of breach clause into the governing law/severability section obfuscates the immediate responsibility to notify the disclosing party. This relocation reduces the clarity of the obligation and its prominence.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 201 CMR 17.00 Standards for The Protection of Personal Information of Residents of the Commonwealth
perturbation.law_explanation: 201 CMR 17.03(1) requires a comprehensive information security program. By obscuring the breach notification clause and placing it within the general legal provisions, the agreement fails to adequately emphasize the importance of immediate action upon a breach, potentially hindering compliance with the mandatory data breach notification requirements outlined in Massachusetts law.

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by obscuring the breach notification clause, making it less prominent and potentially delaying or hindering the recipient's obligation to promptly notify the disclosing party of a breach. The relocation of this clause within the general legal provisions diminishes its significance. As the law explanation states, 201 CMR 17.03(1) requires a comprehensive information security program. By obscuring the breach notification clause and placing it within the general legal provisions, the agreement fails to adequately emphasize the importance of immediate action upon a breach. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that 201 CMR 17.00 establishes minimum standards to be met by persons who own or license personal information about a resident of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts in connection with the safeguarding of personal information. By obscuring the breach notification obligation, the modified contract undermines these minimum standards for safeguarding personal information, creating a contradiction. This falls under the definition of Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction because a required legal disclosure is placed in a misleading section.

perturbation.explanation: Relocating the export control clause to the section on interpretation reduces its visibility and emphasis, making it less likely to be properly considered and adhered to. Export control requirements should be prominently displayed, not hidden within definitions.
perturbation.law_explanation: 15 CFR § 732.1 outlines the steps for determining export control requirements. Moving the clause addressing export-controlled information into the 'Interpretation' section of the contract obfuscates this important responsibility. This obfuscation of these compliance requirements could lead to violations of export control laws, because it deemphasizes the necessity of knowing about the EAR.

perturbation.justification: The changed text relocates the export control clause to the agreement's interpretation section. The perturbation explanation notes this reduces its visibility and emphasis, making adherence less likely. The law explanation supports this by stating that moving the clause 'obfuscates this important responsibility' violating 15 CFR § 732.1 which outlines determining export control requirements. The scraped law snippet 1 from 15 CFR Part 732 reinforces the importance of these regulations under the Bureau of Industry and Security, Department of Commerce. Because the altered text decreases the likelihood of adhering to export control regulations, and therefore decreases the likelihood of compliance with 15 CFR Part 732, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The disclosure of Confidential Information shall not constitute any representation, warranty, assurance, or guarantee, with respect to non -infringement of any patent or other pr oprietary rights of others. NOTWITHSTANDING ANY PROVISION IN THIS AGREEMENT , NEITHER PARTY MAKES ANY REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES AS TO THE COMPLETENESS, ACCURACY, MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR USE FOR ANY PURPOSE OF THE INFORMATION DISCLOSED UNDER THIS AGREEMENT, WHICH IS DELIVERED “AS IS” . EXCEPT AS SET FORTH IN SECTION 7 OF THIS AGREEMENT AND IN THE CASES OF FRAUD OR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, THE DISCLOSING PARTY SHALL HAVE NO LIABILITY FOR ANY DIRECT OR INDIRECT DAMAGES WHICH MAY ARISE FROM THE RECIPIENT’S POSSESSION OR USE OF SUCH INFORMATION.

perturbation.explanation: By moving the clause excluding liability for damages (except for fraud) into the interpretation section, the agreement significantly reduces the prominence of this critical limitation of liability. This makes it less apparent to a party reviewing the agreement and could be seen as misleading.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 2-316 addresses the exclusion or modification of warranties, and implies that disclaimers of liability must be conspicuous and clear. While this agreement deals with information, the principle of clear and conspicuous disclaimers is applicable. Placing the liability disclaimer in the 'Interpretation' section renders it less conspicuous, potentially undermining its effectiveness and contradicting the spirit of UCC § 2-316 which aims to protect parties from unknowingly waiving their rights.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-316. Exclusion or Modification of Warranties. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-316. Exclusion or Modification of Warranties. (1) Words or conduct relevant to the creation of an express warranty and words or conduct tending to negate or limit warranty shall be construed wherever reasonable as consistent with each other; but subject to the provisions of this Article on parol or extrinsic evidence (Section 2-202 ) negation or limitation is inoperative to the extent that such construction is unreasonable. (2)Subject to subsection (3), to exclude or modify the implied warranty of merchantability or any part of it the language must mention merchantability and in case of a writing must be conspicuous, and to exclude or modify any implied warranty of fitness the exclusion must be by a writing and conspicuous. Language to exclude all implied warranties of fitness is sufficient if it states, for example, that "There are no warranties which extend beyond the description on the face hereof." (3)Notwithstanding subsection (2) (a) unless the circumstances indicate otherwise, all implied warranties are excluded by expressions like "as is", "with all faults" or other language which in common understanding calls the buyer's attention to the exclusion of warranties and makes plain that there is no implied warranty; and (b) when the buyer before entering into the contract has examined the goods or the sample or model as fully as he desired or has refused to examine the goods there is no implied warranty with regard to defects which an examination ought in the circumstances to have revealed to him; and (c) an implied warranty can also be excluded or modified by course of dealing or course of performance or usage of trade. (4) Remedies for breach of warranty can be limited in accordance with the provisions of this Article on liquidation or limitation of damages and on contractual modification of remed
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text contradicts the spirit, if not the letter, of UCC § 2-316, concerning the conspicuousness of warranty disclaimers. By moving the liability disclaimer into the 'Interpretation' section, the agreement buries it, making it less obvious to the recipient, which undermines the principle that disclaimers should be conspicuous as described in UCC § 2-316. While the scraped law snippet directly refers to warranties and this agreement concerns confidential information, the law explanation reasonably extends the principle of conspicuousness to liability disclaimers, which is undermined here. The Structural Flaws perturbation definition is triggered because a critical legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret.
